questions.prompt: What is the correct order of elements in a well-formed Spanish argument?
questions.explanation: The standard structure is: tesis (claim) → argumentos (support) → contraargumento (opposing view) → refutación (rebuttal) → conclusión.

questions.prompt: Which device repeats a phrase at the start of successive clauses for emphasis?
questions.explanation: Anáfora (anaphora) is the repetition of a word or phrase at the start of successive clauses to create emphasis and rhythm.

questions.prompt: Which connector introduces a conclusion in formal writing?
questions.explanation: 'Por todo lo expuesto' (given all of the above) is a classic formal conclusion marker. 'Ahora bien' signals rebuttal, 'si bien es cierto' signals concession, and 'hay que reconocer que' signals concession.

questions.prompt: '¿Acaso podemos ignorar lo que está pasando?' is an example of:
questions.explanation: A pregunta retórica (rhetorical question) is posed for effect and does not expect a literal answer. '¿Acaso...?' is a common marker of rhetorical questions in Spanish.

questions.prompt: Complete: '_______ que la medida tiene limitaciones, es el mejor camino disponible.'
questions.explanation: 'Reconozco que...' (I acknowledge that...) introduces a concession — admitting a limitation before proceeding to defend the position.

questions.prompt: Which sentence demonstrates the antithesis rhetorical device?
options: Necesitamos más recursos. Necesitamos más tiempo. Necesitamos más apoyo.
No se trata de tener razón, sino de encontrar la verdad.
En definitiva, la reforma es necesaria e inaplazable.
Si bien es cierto que hay obstáculos, estos son superables.
questions.explanation: Antithesis juxtaposes contrasting ideas in parallel structure. 'No se trata de tener razón, sino de encontrar la verdad' contrasts two opposing concepts deliberately.

questions.prompt: Why is acknowledging a counterargument considered a sign of strong argumentation?
options: It weakens your position by admitting doubt
It is required by academic style guides
It demonstrates intellectual honesty and makes your own position stronger
It adds length to make the essay more formal
questions.explanation: Acknowledging opposing views shows intellectual honesty and signals that your conclusion holds even when challenged — making your argument more persuasive, not weaker.

questions.prompt: The Mexican tradition of political debate mentioned in the lesson is rooted in which historical era?
options: The Reform era of the 19th century
questions.explanation: The lesson specifically mentions the Reform (La Reforma) era of the 19th century as producing some of the finest political oratory in Latin American history, notably associated with Benito Juárez.
